title: Joshua 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 After Moses, the servant of the LORD, died, the LORD spoke to Joshua son of Nun. Joshua had been Moses' helper. The LORD said,
2 "My servant Moses is dead. Now get up and cross the Jordan River. Take all these people with you. Go into the land that I am giving to the children of Israel.
3 I have given you every place where your foot steps, just as I promised Moses.
4 Your land will reach from the desert and Lebanon to the great Euphrates River. It will include all the land of the Hittites. It will reach west to the Great Sea.
5 No one will be able to stop you all the days of your life. I was with Moses, and I will be with you. I will never leave you or turn away from you.
6 **Be strong and courageous.** You will lead these people to take the land. I promised their fathers long ago that I would give it to them.
7 Most of all, **be strong and courageous.** Be careful to obey all the law that my servant Moses gave you. Do not turn away from it at all. Then you will do well wherever you go.
8 Always speak the words of this Book of the Law. Think deeply about it day and night. Then you will be careful to do everything written in it. Then you will do well and have success in all you do.
9 **Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God will be with you wherever you go.**"
10 Then Joshua gave orders to the leaders of the people.

11 He said, "Go through the camp and tell the people, 'Get your food and supplies ready. In three days you will cross the Jordan River. You will go in and take the land that the LORD your God is giving you.'"
12 Then Joshua spoke to the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and half of Manasseh. He said,
13 "Remember what Moses the servant of the LORD told you. He said, 'The LORD your God will give you rest. He will give you this land.'

14 Your wives, your young children, and your animals may stay here. They will stay in the land that Moses gave you east of the Jordan. But all your strong soldiers must get ready for war with weapons. They must cross over ahead of their brothers and help them.

15 Help them until the LORD gives them rest, just as he gave you rest. Help them until your brothers also take the land that the LORD your God is giving them. Then you may return to your own land. You may take the land that Moses the servant of the LORD gave you east of the Jordan."
16 So they answered Joshua, "We will do everything you have told us. We will go wherever you send us.
17 We obeyed Moses in all things. We will obey you in the same way. And may the LORD your God be with you, as he was with Moses.
18 Anyone who refuses to obey your orders will be killed. Anyone who does not do what you say will be killed. Most of all, **be strong and courageous!**"

title: Joshua 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then Joshua son of Nun quietly sent two spies from Shittim. He said, "Go and look at the land, especially Jericho." So they went and entered the house of a prostitute named Rahab. They stayed there.
2 Someone told the king of Jericho, "Look, some men of Israel have come here tonight to spy out the land."
3 So the king of Jericho sent word to Rahab. He said, "Bring out the men who came to your house. They have come to spy out the whole land."
4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. She said, "Yes, the men did come to me. But I did not know where they came from.
5 When it was getting dark, the gate was about to close. The men went out. I do not know which way they went. Chase them quickly! You may catch them."
6 (But Rahab had taken them up to the roof. She had hidden them under flax plants she had laid out there.)
7 So the king's men ran after the spies. They went along the road to the crossing places of the Jordan. As soon as they left, the gate was shut.
8 Before the spies lay down for the night, Rahab went up to the roof.
9 She said to them, "I know that the LORD has given you this land. Great fear of you has come on us. All who live in the land are losing all courage because of you.

10 For we have heard how the LORD dried up the Red Sea before you when you came out of Egypt. We heard what you did to Sihon and Og, the two kings of the Amorites across the Jordan, whom you fully destroyed.
11 When we heard this, our hearts melted. Everyone's courage was gone because of you. For **the LORD your God is God in heaven above and on the earth below**.
12 Now please promise me by the LORD. You will be kind to my family, because I have been kind to you. Give me a true sign
13 that you will save the lives of my father and mother, my brothers and sisters, and all who belong to them. Save us from death."
14 "Our lives for your lives!" the men agreed. "If you do not tell about our mission, we will be kind and faithful to you when the LORD gives us the land."
15 Then Rahab let them down with a rope through the window. Her house was built into the wall of the city.
16 "Go to the hills," she said, "so the men chasing you will not find you. Hide there for three days until they return. Then go on your way."
17 The men said to her, "We will be free from this promise you made us swear,

18 unless you do this. When we enter the land, you must tie this scarlet cord in the window. It is the one you let us down through. You must also bring your father and mother and brothers and all your family into your house.

19 If anyone goes out the door of your house into the street, his death will be his own fault. We will not be guilty. But if anyone hurts a person who stays with you in the house, his death will be our fault.
20 And if you tell about our mission, we will be free from the promise you made us swear."
21 "Let it be as you say," she replied. Then she sent them away. After they had gone, she tied the scarlet cord in the window.
22 So the spies went out into the hills. They stayed there three days, until the men chasing them returned. The men had searched all along the road but did not find them.
23 Then the two men started back. They came down from the hills and crossed the river. They came to Joshua son of Nun and told him all that had happened.
24 "The LORD has truly given the whole land into our hands," they said to Joshua. "Indeed, all who live in the land are losing all courage because of us."

title: Joshua 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Early the next morning, Joshua got up. He left Shittim with all the people of Israel. They went as far as the Jordan. They camped there before crossing over.
2 After three days, the leaders went through the camp.
3 They told the people, "Watch for the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God. The priests from the tribe of Levi will carry it. When you see it, leave your places and follow it.
4 But keep about 900 meters between you and the ark. Do not go near it. This way, you will see the path to take. You have never traveled this way before."
5 Then Joshua told the people, "Make yourselves holy. Tomorrow the LORD will do great things among you."
6 Joshua said to the priests, "Take the ark of the covenant. Go ahead of the people." So they took the ark and went ahead of them.
7 Then the LORD said to Joshua, "Today I will begin to lift you up. All Israel will see it. They will know that I am with you, just as I was with Moses.
8 Tell the priests who carry the ark of the covenant, 'When you reach the edge of the water, stand still in the Jordan.'"
9 So Joshua said to the people of Israel, "Come here. Listen to the words of the LORD your God."
10 He went on, "This is how you will know the living God is among you. He will surely push out before you the Canaanites, Hittites, Hivites, Perizzites, Girgashites, Amorites, and Jebusites.
11 Look — the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth will go ahead of you into the Jordan.
12 Now choose twelve men from the tribes of Israel. Pick one man from each tribe.
13 The priests will carry the ark of the LORD, the Lord of all the earth. When their feet touch the water of the Jordan, its flowing water will stop. The water will pile up in one place."
14 So the people left the camp to cross the Jordan. The priests carried the ark of the covenant ahead of them.
15 The Jordan flows over its sides during the time of harvest. But the priests came to the Jordan carrying the ark. As soon as their feet touched the edge of the water,

16 the flowing water stood still. It backed up far upstream as far as Adam, a city near Zarethan. The water going down to the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea) was completely cut off. So the people crossed over near Jericho.

17 The priests carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD. They stood still on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan. All Israel crossed over on dry ground. They went across until the whole nation had crossed the Jordan.

title: Joshua 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 All the people finished crossing the Jordan. Then the LORD said to Joshua,
2 "Choose twelve men from the people, one from each tribe.
3 Tell them, 'Take twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan. Take them from where the priests are standing. Carry them with you. Put them down where you sleep tonight.'"
4 So Joshua called the twelve men he had chosen from Israel. He chose one from each tribe.

5 He said to them, "Cross over before the ark of the LORD your God. Go into the middle of the Jordan. Each of you must lift a stone onto his shoulder. There will be one stone for each tribe of Israel.
6 These stones will be a sign for you. In the future, your children will ask, 'What do these stones mean to you?'

7 Then you will tell them, 'The water of the Jordan stopped flowing. It stopped before the ark of the covenant of the LORD. When the ark crossed the Jordan, the water stopped.' So these stones will always remind Israel of this."

8 The Israelites did what Joshua told them. They took twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan. They took one stone for each tribe of Israel. They did this just as the LORD told Joshua. They carried the stones to the camp and put them down there.
9 Joshua also set up twelve stones in the middle of the Jordan. He put them where the priests with the ark of the covenant stood. The stones are still there today.

10 The priests with the ark kept standing in the middle of the Jordan. They stood there until the people finished everything the LORD told Joshua to say. Moses had also told Joshua to do this. The people crossed quickly.
11 When everyone finished crossing, the priests with the ark of the LORD crossed too. The people watched them cross.
12 The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh crossed first. They went before the other Israelites. They were ready for battle, just as Moses had told them.
13 About 40,000 soldiers ready for battle crossed before the LORD. They crossed into the plains of Jericho.
14 That day the LORD made Joshua great in front of all Israel. The people respected him all his life, just as they had respected Moses.
15 Then the LORD said to Joshua,
16 "Tell the priests who carry the ark of the Testimony. Tell them to come up from the Jordan."
17 So Joshua told the priests, "Come up from the Jordan."

18 The priests carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD. They came up out of the Jordan. Their feet touched the dry land. Then the water of the Jordan flowed again. It filled the river to the top, just as before.
19 On the tenth day of the first month, the people went up from the Jordan. They camped at Gilgal, on the eastern border of Jericho.
20 At Gilgal, Joshua set up the twelve stones they had taken from the Jordan.
21 Then Joshua said to the Israelites, "In the future, your children will ask their fathers, 'What do these stones mean?'
22 Then you will tell them, 'Israel crossed the Jordan on dry ground.'
23 The LORD your God dried up the water of the Jordan for you. He dried it up until you finished crossing. He did the same thing at the Red Sea. He dried it up for us until we finished crossing.
24 He did this so all the peoples of the earth would know. They will know that the LORD's hand is strong. And you will always fear the LORD your God."

title: Joshua 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 The Amorite kings west of the Jordan heard the news. The Canaanite kings near the sea also heard it. They learned that the LORD had dried up the Jordan for the people of Israel. So they lost all courage and felt afraid of Israel.
2 At that time the LORD said to Joshua, "Make stone knives. Circumcise the sons of Israel again."
3 So Joshua made stone knives. He circumcised the sons of Israel at Gibeath-haaraloth.
4 This is why Joshua circumcised them. All the fighting men who came out of Egypt had died. They died on the journey in the desert after they left Egypt.
5 All the men who came out of Egypt were circumcised. But the men born in the desert on the journey were not.

6 The people of Israel walked in the desert for forty years. All the fighting men who came out of Egypt died there. They did not obey the LORD. So the LORD promised they would not see the land. He had sworn to their fathers to give us a land full of milk and honey.
7 Joshua raised up their sons in their place. He circumcised these sons. They had not been circumcised on the way.
8 After the whole nation was circumcised, they stayed in the camp. They rested there until they were healed.
9 Then the LORD said to Joshua, "Today I have rolled away the shame of Egypt from you." So that place is called Gilgal to this day.
10 The people of Israel camped at Gilgal on the plains of Jericho. On the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, they kept the Passover.
11 The day after the Passover, they ate food from the land. They ate bread made without yeast and roasted grain.
12 The next day, the manna stopped. There was no more manna for the people of Israel. That year they ate the crops of the land of Canaan.
13 Joshua was near Jericho. He looked up and saw a man standing in front of him. The man had a sword ready in His hand. Joshua walked up to Him and asked, "Are You for us or for our enemies?"
14 "Neither," He answered. "I have come as Commander of the LORD's army." Then Joshua bowed low to the ground. He asked Him, "What does my Lord want to say to His servant?"
15 The Commander of the LORD's army replied, "Take off your sandals. The place where you are standing is holy." And Joshua did so.

title: Joshua 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Jericho was shut up tight because of the Israelites. No one went out or came in.
2 The LORD said to Joshua, "Look, I have given Jericho into your hand. Its king and strong fighters are yours.
3 March around the city with all your fighting men. Go around it one time. Do this for six days.
4 Seven priests will carry seven horns from rams in front of the ark. On the seventh day, march around the city seven times. The priests will blow the horns.

5 Then the priests will make a long sound from the horn. When you hear it, all the people must shout very loudly. The wall of the city will fall down flat. Then your people will rush straight into the city."
6 So Joshua son of Nun called the priests. He said, "Take up the ark of the covenant. Have seven priests carry seven horns from rams in front of the ark."
7 He told the people, "Go forward and march around the city. The armed soldiers must go ahead of the ark of the LORD."
8 After Joshua spoke, seven priests with seven horns from rams went before the LORD. They blew the horns, and the ark of the covenant followed them.
9 The armed soldiers walked ahead of the priests who blew the horns. The back guard followed the ark. The horns kept sounding.
10 Joshua had told the people, "Do not shout. Do not let your voice be heard. Do not say one word until I tell you to shout. Then you must shout!"
11 So he had the ark of the LORD carried around the city one time. Then the people returned to camp and stayed the night.
12 Joshua got up early the next morning. The priests took up the ark of the LORD.
13 Seven priests with seven horns from rams kept marching in front of the ark. They blew the horns. Armed soldiers went before them, and the back guard followed the ark. The horns kept sounding.
14 So on the second day they marched around the city one time. Then they returned to camp. They did this for six days.
15 On the seventh day, they got up at dawn. They marched around the city seven times in the same way. That was the only day they went around seven times.
16 After the seventh time, the priests blew the horns. Joshua said to the people, "Shout! The LORD has given you the city!
17 The city and everything in it must be devoted to the LORD for destruction. Only Rahab and all who are with her in her house will live. She hid the spies we sent.
18 Keep away from the things devoted to destruction. If you take any of them, you will bring trouble on the whole camp of Israel.
19 All the silver, gold, bronze, and iron are holy to the LORD. They must go into His storeroom."

20 So when the horns sounded, the people shouted very loudly. When they heard the long sound from the horn, they gave a great shout. The wall fell down flat. Then all the people rushed straight into the city and took it.
21 With the sword they devoted everything in the city to destruction — men and women, young and old, cows, sheep, and donkeys.
22 Joshua said to the two men who had spied out the land, "Go into the house of Rahab. Bring out the woman and all who are with her, just as you promised her."

23 So the young spies went in. They brought out Rahab, her father, her mother, her brothers, and all who belonged to her. They brought out her whole family and put them in a safe place outside the camp.
24 Then the Israelites burned the city and everything in it. But they put the silver, gold, bronze, and iron into the storeroom of the LORD's house.
25 Joshua saved Rahab, her father's family, and all who belonged to her. She hid the men Joshua sent to spy out Jericho. So she has lived among the Israelites to this day.

26 At that time Joshua said this serious oath: "Under God's curse before the LORD is the man who rebuilds this city, Jericho. He will lose his oldest son when he lays its foundation. He will lose his youngest son when he sets up its gates."
27 So the LORD was with Joshua. People heard about him all through the land.

title: Joshua 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 But the Israelites were not faithful about the things set apart for the LORD. Achan, son of Carmi, took some of these things. Carmi was the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, from the tribe of Judah. So the LORD was very angry with the Israelites.
2 Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai. Ai is near Beth-aven, east of Bethel. He told them, "Go up and look at the land in secret." So the men went up and looked at Ai.
3 They came back to Joshua and said, "You do not need to send all the people. Two or three thousand men are enough to attack Ai. The people of Ai are few. Do not make all our people tired there."
4 So about three thousand men went up. But they ran away from the men of Ai.

5 The men of Ai killed about thirty-six of them. They chased them from the gate to the stone pits. They struck them down on the slopes. So the people lost their courage. Their hearts became like water.
6 Then Joshua tore his clothes. He fell with his face to the ground before the ark of the LORD. He stayed there until evening. The leaders of Israel did the same. They all put dust on their heads.

7 Joshua said, "O Lord GOD, why did you bring this people across the Jordan? Did you bring us here to give us over to the Amorites? Did you bring us here to be destroyed? We should have been happy to stay on the other side of the Jordan!
8 O Lord, what can I say now? Israel has turned away and run from its enemies.
9 The Canaanites and all who live in the land will hear about this. They will come around us and take away our name from the earth. Then what will you do for your great name?"
10 But the LORD said to Joshua, "Stand up! Why have you fallen on your face?

11 Israel has sinned. They have broken my covenant that I gave them. They have taken some of the things set apart for me. They have stolen and lied. They have hidden these things with their own things.

12 This is why the Israelites cannot stand against their enemies. They turn their backs and run away. They themselves have become set apart for destruction. I will not be with you anymore. First you must remove these things from among you.

13 Get up! Make the people holy. Say to them, 'Make yourselves holy for tomorrow. The LORD, the God of Israel, says this: There are things set apart for me among you, Israel. You cannot stand against your enemies until you remove them.

14 In the morning, come forward one tribe at a time. The tribe that the LORD chooses must come forward one clan at a time. The clan that the LORD chooses must come forward one family at a time. The family that the LORD chooses must come forward one man at a time.
15 The one caught with these things must be burned. All that he owns must be burned too. He has broken the covenant of the LORD. He has done a shameful thing in Israel.'"
16 So Joshua got up early the next morning. He had Israel come forward one tribe at a time. The tribe of Judah was chosen.
17 He had the clans of Judah come forward. The clan of the Zerahites was chosen. He had the clan of the Zerahites come forward. The family of Zabdi was chosen.
18 He had the family of Zabdi come forward one man at a time. Achan was chosen. Achan was the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, from the tribe of Judah.
19 Joshua said to Achan, "My son, give honor to the LORD, the God of Israel. Tell him what you have done. Please tell me what you did. Do not hide it from me."
20 Achan answered, "It is true. I have sinned against the LORD, the God of Israel. This is what I did.

21 I saw a beautiful coat from Shinar among the things our army took. I also saw 200 shekels of silver and a bar of gold weighing 50 shekels. I strongly wanted them and took them. They are hidden in the ground inside my tent. The silver is at the bottom."
22 So Joshua sent messengers. They ran to the tent. The things were there, hidden in his tent. The silver was at the bottom.
23 They took the things from the tent. They brought them to Joshua and all the Israelites. They spread them out before the LORD.

24 Then Joshua and all Israel took Achan son of Zerah. They also took the silver, the coat, the bar of gold, his sons and daughters, his oxen, donkeys, and sheep. They took his tent and everything he owned. They brought them all to the Valley of Achor.

25 Joshua said, "Why have you brought this trouble on us? Today the LORD will bring trouble on you!" Then all Israel threw stones at him and killed him. They also stoned the others and burned their bodies.
26 They put a big pile of rocks over Achan. The pile is still there today. So the LORD turned away from his strong anger. That is why that place is called the Valley of Achor today.

title: Joshua 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Joshua, "Do not be afraid or afraid to fight. Take your whole army and go attack Ai. Look, I have given you the king of Ai, his people, his city, and his land."
2 "Do to Ai and its king what you did to Jericho and its king. But this time you may keep the goods and animals. Set a hidden group of soldiers behind the city."
3 So Joshua and the whole army set out to attack Ai. He chose 30,000 strong, brave soldiers and sent them out at night.
4 He gave them these orders: "Listen carefully. Hide behind the city, but not too far away. All of you must be ready."
5 "I and the soldiers with me will march toward the city. When they come out to fight us, we will run away."
6 "They will chase us until we have drawn them away from the city. They will say, 'The Israelites are running away again.' So we will keep running."
7 "Then you must rise from your hiding place and take the city. The LORD your God will give it into your hand."
8 "When you have taken the city, burn it. Do what the LORD has commanded. Look, I have given you my orders."
9 So Joshua sent them out, and they hid between Bethel and Ai, west of Ai. But Joshua stayed with the people that night.
10 Early the next morning, Joshua got up and got his soldiers ready. He and the leaders of Israel marched in front of them up to Ai.
11 All the soldiers with him marched up and came near the city. They camped to the north of Ai, with the valley between them and the city.
12 Joshua had also taken about 5,000 men and hid them between Bethel and Ai, west of the city.
13 So the main camp was north of the city and the hidden group was west of it. That night Joshua went into the valley.
14 When the king of Ai saw the Israelites, he hurried out early with his men to fight them at a chosen place above the Arabah valley. But he did not know that soldiers were hidden behind the city.
15 Joshua and all Israel pretended to lose, and they ran away toward the empty land.
16 Then all the men of Ai were called to chase them. They followed Joshua and were drawn away from the city.
17 Not one man was left in Ai or Bethel. They all went out to chase Israel, leaving the city wide open.
18 Then the LORD said to Joshua, "Hold out your spear toward Ai. I will give the city into your hand." So Joshua held out his spear toward Ai.
19 As soon as he did this, the hidden men jumped up from their place. They rushed forward, entered the city, took it, and quickly set it on fire.
20 When the men of Ai turned and looked back, they saw smoke rising into the sky. They could not escape anywhere. The Israelites who had run away now turned to chase them.
21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the hidden men had taken the city and smoke was rising, they turned around and killed the men of Ai.
22 The hidden men also came out of the city to fight them. The men of Ai were trapped between the two Israelite groups. So Israel killed them until no one escaped alive.
23 But they took the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
24 Israel killed all the men of Ai who had chased them into the open land. Every one of them fell by the sword. Then all the Israelites returned to Ai and killed everyone there.
25 That day 12,000 men and women died — all the people of Ai.
26 Joshua did not pull back the hand that held his spear until he had fully destroyed all who lived in Ai.
27 Israel kept for themselves only the animals and goods of the city, as the LORD had commanded Joshua.
28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a pile of broken stones forever, an empty place to this day.

29 He hung the king of Ai on a tree until evening. At sunset Joshua ordered them to take the body down from the tree. They threw it down at the city gate. Then they piled large rocks over it, and the pile is still there today.
30 At that time Joshua built an altar to the LORD, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal.

31 He built it just as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded. He followed what is written in the Book of the Law of Moses: "an altar of uncut stones, with no iron tool used on them." On it they offered burnt offerings to the LORD, and they offered peace offerings.
32 There, in front of the Israelites, Joshua wrote on the stones a copy of the law of Moses.

33 All Israel, both foreigners and citizens, with their leaders, officers, and judges, stood on both sides of the ark of the covenant of the LORD. They faced the Levite priests who carried it. Half of the people stood in front of Mount Gerizim and half in front of Mount Ebal. Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded this earlier, to bless the people of Israel.
34 After this, Joshua read aloud all the words of the law — the blessings and the curses — just as they are written in the Book of the Law.
35 Joshua read every word that Moses had commanded. He read it before the whole gathering of Israel, including the women, the children, and the foreigners who lived among them.

title: Joshua 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Many kings lived west of the Jordan River. They lived in the hills, the low country, and along the Great Sea.
2 These kings were Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites. They heard the news and came together to fight Joshua and Israel.
3 But the people of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and Ai.
4 So they used a trick. They went as messengers with old, torn bags on their donkeys. They also took old wine bags that were cracked and fixed.
5 They wore old, patched sandals and very old clothes. All their bread was dry and moldy.
6 They came to Joshua at the camp in Gilgal. They said to Joshua and the men of Israel, "We have come from a far land. Please make a peace agreement with us."
7 But the men of Israel said to the Hivites, "Maybe you live near us. How can we make a peace agreement with you?"
8 They said to Joshua, "We are your servants." Joshua asked them, "Who are you? Where have you come from?"
9 They answered, "Your servants have come from a very far land. We came because of the great name of the LORD your God. We heard reports about him and all he did in Egypt.
10 We also heard what he did to the two kings of the Amorites east of the Jordan. He defeated Sihon, king of Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth.
11 So our elders and our people told us, 'Take food and supplies for the journey. Go and meet them. Say to them: We are your servants. Please make a peace agreement with us.'
12 This bread was warm when we packed it at home on the day we left. But look, now it is dry and moldy.
13 These wine bags were new when we filled them. But look, they are cracked. Our clothes and sandals are worn out from the long journey."
14 The men of Israel tasted their food. But they did not ask the LORD for advice.
15 Joshua made a peace agreement with them and let them live. The leaders of Israel made a strong promise to them.
16 Three days later, the Israelites learned the truth. The Gibeonites were neighbors who lived nearby.
17 So the Israelites went out. On the third day they came to their cities — Gibeon, Chephirah, Beeroth, and Kiriath-jearim.
18 But the Israelites did not attack them. The leaders had made a strong promise by the LORD, the God of Israel. All the people complained against the leaders.
19 All the leaders answered, "We made a strong promise to them by the LORD, the God of Israel. So we cannot touch them now.
20 This is what we will do: We will let them live. Then no anger will fall on us because of our promise."
21 The leaders also said, "Let them live. But they will cut wood and carry water for all the people." So the leaders kept their promise.
22 Then Joshua called the Gibeonites. He said, "Why did you trick us? You told us you live far away, but you live among us.
23 So now you are cursed. You will always serve as men who cut wood and carry water for the house of my God."

24 The Gibeonites answered, "Your servants heard a clear report. The LORD your God told his servant Moses to give you all the land. He also told you to destroy all the people who lived here. So we feared for our lives. That is why we did this.
25 Now we are in your hands. Do to us whatever you think is good and right."
26 So Joshua did this. He saved them from the hands of the Israelites, and they did not kill the Gibeonites.
27 On that day, Joshua made them men who cut wood and carry water. They still do this work today for the people and for the altar of the LORD at the place he would choose.

title: Joshua 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Adoni-zedek, king of Jerusalem, heard the news about Joshua. Joshua had captured Ai and destroyed it completely. He had done to Ai and its king what he did to Jericho and its king. Also, the people of Gibeon had made peace with Israel and lived near them.
2 So Adoni-zedek and his people were very afraid. Gibeon was a great city, like a city of kings. It was bigger than Ai, and all its men were strong fighters.
3 So Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent a message to Hoham king of Hebron, Piram king of Jarmuth, Japhia king of Lachish, and Debir king of Eglon. He said,
4 "Come up and help me. We will attack Gibeon, because they have made peace with Joshua and the Israelites."

5 So the five kings of the Amorites joined their armies together. They were the kings of Jerusalem, Hebron, Jarmuth, Lachish, and Eglon. They marched out with all their soldiers. They set up camp near Gibeon and made war against it.

6 Then the men of Gibeon sent a message to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal. They said, "Do not leave us, your servants. Come quickly and save us! Help us, because all the Amorite kings from the mountain area have joined against us."
7 So Joshua went up from Gilgal with his whole army, with all the brave strong soldiers.
8 The LORD said to Joshua, "Do not be afraid of them. I have given them to you. Not one of them will stand against you."
9 Joshua marched all night from Gilgal. Then he surprised them with a sudden attack.

10 And the LORD made the enemies confused before Israel. Israel defeated them in a great defeat at Gibeon. They chased them along the road up to Beth-horon. They struck them down as far as Azekah and Makkedah.

11 As the enemies ran away from Israel on the road down from Beth-horon to Azekah, the LORD sent big hailstones down on them from the sky. More of them died from the hailstones than from the swords of the Israelites.
12 On the day that the LORD gave the Amorites to the Israelites, Joshua spoke to the LORD in front of Israel. He said: **"Sun, stand still over Gibeon, and you, moon, over the Valley of Aijalon."**

13 So the sun stood still, and the moon stopped, until the nation paid back its enemies. Is this not written in the Book of Jashar? **"The sun stopped in the middle of the sky and delayed going down about a full day. There has never been a day like it before or since"** —
14 when the LORD listened to the voice of a man, because the LORD fought for Israel.
15 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.
16 Now the five kings had run away and hidden in the cave at Makkedah.
17 Someone told Joshua, "The five kings have been found. They are hiding in the cave at Makkedah."
18 So Joshua said, "Roll large stones against the mouth of the cave. Put men there to guard it.
19 But you, do not stop there. Chase your enemies and attack them from behind. Do not let them reach their cities. The LORD your God has given them to you."
20 So Joshua and the Israelites killed many of them until they had finished them off. The few who survived escaped to the strong walled cities.
21 The whole army returned safely to Joshua in the camp at Makkedah. No one dared to say a word against the Israelites.
22 Then Joshua said, "Open the mouth of the cave. Bring those five kings out to me."
23 So they brought the five kings out of the cave. They were the kings of Jerusalem, Hebron, Jarmuth, Lachish, and Eglon.

24 When they brought the kings to Joshua, he called all the men of Israel. He said to the army leaders who had gone with him, "Come here. Put your feet on the necks of these kings." So the leaders came forward and put their feet on their necks.
25 Joshua said, "Do not be afraid or lose hope. Be strong and brave. The LORD will do this to all the enemies you fight."
26 After this, Joshua struck down and killed the kings. He hung their bodies on five trees and left them there until evening.

27 At sunset, Joshua ordered them to take the bodies down from the trees. They threw them into the cave where the kings had hidden. Then they placed large stones against the mouth of the cave. The stones are still there today.

28 On that day Joshua captured Makkedah. He killed everyone in it with the sword, along with its king. He destroyed everyone in the city completely. No one survived. He did to the king of Makkedah what he had done to the king of Jericho.
29 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Makkedah to Libnah. They fought against Libnah.
30 And the LORD also gave that city and its king to Israel. Joshua killed all the people with the sword. No one survived. He did to the king of Libnah what he had done to the king of Jericho.
31 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Libnah to Lachish. They surrounded it and fought against it.
32 And the LORD gave Lachish to Israel. Joshua captured it on the second day. He killed all the people with the sword, just as he had done to Libnah.
33 At that time, Horam king of Gezer came to help Lachish. But Joshua struck him down along with his people. No one survived.
34 Then Joshua moved on from Lachish to Eglon, and all Israel with him. They surrounded it and fought against it.
35 That day they captured Eglon and killed everyone with the sword. Joshua destroyed everyone in the city, just as he had done to Lachish.
36 Then Joshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron. They fought against it.

37 They captured it and killed its king with the sword. They also killed all its villages and all the people. Joshua left no survivors, just as he had done at Eglon. He destroyed Hebron and everyone in it completely.
38 Finally Joshua and all Israel with him turned toward Debir. They fought against it.

39 They captured Debir, its king, and all its villages. They killed them with the sword. They destroyed everyone in the city completely. No one survived. Joshua did to Debir and its king what he had done to Hebron and to Libnah and its king.

40 So Joshua took the whole region. This included the mountain area, the Negev, the low hills, and the slopes, with all their kings. He left no survivors. He destroyed every living person, just as the LORD, the God of Israel, had commanded.
41 Joshua took the land from Kadesh-barnea to Gaza. He also took the whole region of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
42 The LORD, the God of Israel, fought for Israel. So Joshua captured all these kings and their land in one war.
43 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.

title: Joshua 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Now Jabin king of Hazor heard about these things. He sent a message to Jobab king of Madon, and to the kings of Shimron and Achshaph.
2 He also sent word to the kings in the north — in the mountains, in the Arabah valley south of Chinnereth, in the low hills, and in Naphoth-dor to the west.
3 He sent to the Canaanites in the east and west, to the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, and Jebusites in the hill country, and to the Hivites at the foot of Hermon in the land of Mizpah.
4 So these kings came out with all their armies. They were as many as the sand by the sea, with many horses and war wagons.
5 All these kings joined together. They set up camp at the waters of Merom to fight against Israel.
6 Then the LORD said to Joshua, "Do not be afraid of them. By this time tomorrow, I will hand them all over to Israel, killed. You must cut the leg tendons of their horses and burn their war wagons."
7 So Joshua and his whole army reached them by surprise at the waters of Merom. They attacked them.

8 The LORD gave them into the hand of Israel. Israel killed them and chased them all the way to Greater Sidon and Misrephoth-maim. They chased them east to the Valley of Mizpeh. They killed them and left no one alive.
9 Joshua did what the LORD told him. He cut the leg tendons of their horses and burned their war wagons.
10 At that time Joshua turned back and took Hazor. He killed its king with the sword. Hazor had been the head of all these kingdoms.
11 The Israelites killed everyone in Hazor with the sword. They gave them over to be destroyed. No living thing was left. Then Joshua burned Hazor itself.
12 Joshua took all these kings and their cities. He killed them with the sword. He gave them over to be destroyed, as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded.
13 But Israel did not burn the cities built on their mounds, except Hazor. Joshua burned only Hazor.
14 The Israelites took for themselves all the goods and farm animals of these cities. But they killed all the people with the sword. They left no one alive.
15 The LORD commanded his servant Moses. Moses commanded Joshua. And Joshua did everything. He did all that the LORD had commanded Moses.
16 So Joshua took the whole region: the hill country, all the Negev, all the land of Goshen, the western low hills, the Arabah, and the mountains of Israel with their low hills.
17 He took the land from Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir, as far as Baal-gad in the Valley of Lebanon at the foot of Mount Hermon. He captured all their kings and put them to death.
18 Joshua fought war against all these kings for a long time.
19 No city made peace with the Israelites, except the Hivites who lived in Gibeon. All other cities were taken in battle.
20 The LORD made their hearts hard. So they fought against Israel. He gave them over to be destroyed. They received no mercy. They were wiped out, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

21 At that time Joshua went and removed the Anakim from the hill country. He removed them from Hebron, Debir, and Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah and Israel. Joshua gave them and their cities over to be destroyed.
22 No Anakim were left in the land of the Israelites. Only in Gaza, Gath, and Ashdod did some survive.
23 So Joshua took the whole land. He did all that the LORD had spoken to Moses. Joshua gave the land as a gift to Israel in shares for their tribes. Then the land had peace from war.

title: Joshua 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 These are the kings whom the Israelites defeated east of the Jordan River. The Israelites took their lands. The lands went from the Arnon Valley to Mount Hermon, and all the Arabah Valley to the east:

2 Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon. He ruled from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley. His land went along the middle of the valley to the Jabbok River, the border of the Ammonites. This was half of Gilead.
3 He also ruled the Arabah Valley east of the Sea of Chinnereth. His land reached the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea), east through Beth-jeshimoth, and south below the slopes of Pisgah.
4 And Og king of Bashan, one of the Rephaim left, who lived in Ashtaroth and Edrei.
5 He ruled Mount Hermon, Salecah, and all of Bashan. His land reached the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites. He also ruled half of Gilead, up to the border of Sihon king of Heshbon.
6 Moses, the servant of the LORD, and the Israelites defeated them. Moses gave their land to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh as land to keep.

7 These are the kings whom Joshua and the Israelites defeated west of the Jordan River. The land went from Baal-gad in the Valley of Lebanon to Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir. Joshua gave this land as shares to the tribes of Israel to keep:

8 the hill country, the low hills, the Arabah Valley, the hillsides, the desert, and the southern desert (the Negev). These were the lands of the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
9 the king of Jericho, one; the king of Ai, which is near Bethel, one;
10 the king of Jerusalem, one; the king of Hebron, one;
11 the king of Jarmuth, one; the king of Lachish, one;
12 the king of Eglon, one; the king of Gezer, one;
13 the king of Debir, one; the king of Geder, one;
14 the king of Hormah, one; the king of Arad, one;
15 the king of Libnah, one; the king of Adullam, one;
16 the king of Makkedah, one; the king of Bethel, one;
17 the king of Tappuah, one; the king of Hepher, one;
18 the king of Aphek, one; the king of Lasharon, one;
19 the king of Madon, one; the king of Hazor, one;
20 the king of Shimron-meron, one; the king of Achshaph, one;
21 the king of Taanach, one; the king of Megiddo, one;
22 the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam in Carmel, one;
23 the king of Dor in Naphath-dor, one; the king of Goiim in Gilgal, one;
24 and the king of Tirzah, one. So there were thirty-one kings in all.

title: Joshua 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Now Joshua was very old. The LORD said to him, "You are very old, but much of the land is not yet taken.
2 This is the land that is left:

3 All the land of the Philistines and the Geshurites. It goes from the Shihor east of Egypt to the land of Ekron on the north. People count this as Canaanite land. It includes the five Philistine rulers of Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, and Ekron. It also includes the land of the Avvites;
4 to the south, all the land of the Canaanites. It goes from Mearah of the Sidonians to Aphek, as far as the border of the Amorites;
5 the land of the Gebalites; and all Lebanon to the east. It goes from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon to Lebo-hamath.

6 I myself will push out all the people who live in the hill country. This is from Lebanon to Misrephoth-maim — all the Sidonians. I will push them out before the Israelites. Be sure to divide the land by drawing lots. Give it as land to keep to Israel, as I have told you.
7 So now divide this land as land to keep. Give it to the nine tribes and half of the tribe of Manasseh."

8 The other half of Manasseh, the Reubenites, and the Gadites had already received their land. Moses had given it to them east of the Jordan River. Moses, the servant of the LORD, had given it to them.
9 Their land went from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley. It included the city in the middle of the valley. It included all the high flat land of Medeba as far as Dibon.
10 It included all the cities of Sihon king of the Amorites, who ruled in Heshbon. It went as far as the border of the Ammonites.
11 It also included Gilead and the land of the Geshurites and Maacathites. It included all of Mount Hermon and all Bashan as far as Salecah.
12 It included the whole kingdom of Og in Bashan. Og had ruled as king in Ashtaroth and Edrei. He was one of the Rephaim left. Moses had defeated them and taken their land.
13 But the Israelites did not push out the Geshurites or the Maacathites. So Geshur and Maacath still live among the Israelites today.
14 But Moses gave no land to the tribe of Levi. Their share is the offerings burned in fire to the LORD, the God of Israel. The LORD had promised them this.
15 This is what Moses had given to the family groups of the tribe of Reuben:
16 The land from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley, with the city in the middle of the valley. It included all the high flat land beyond Medeba.
17 It included Heshbon and all its cities on the high flat land. These were Dibon, Bamoth-baal, Beth-baal-meon,
18 Jahaz, Kedemoth, Mephaath,
19 Kiriathaim, Sibmah, Zereth-shahar on the hill in the valley,
20 Beth-peor, the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth —

21 all the cities of the high flat land and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites. Sihon had ruled in Heshbon. Moses killed him and the chiefs of Midian. Those chiefs were Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba. They were the princes of Sihon who lived in the land.
22 The Israelites also killed Balaam son of Beor, the fortune-teller. They killed him with the others by the sword.
23 The border of the Reubenites was the bank of the Jordan River. This was the land to keep for the family groups of the Reubenites. It included the cities and villages.
24 This is what Moses had given to the family groups of the tribe of Gad:
25 The land of Jazer, all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the Ammonites. It went as far as Aroer, near Rabbah.
26 The land went from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim. It also went from Mahanaim to the border of Debir.

27 In the valley, it included Beth-haram, Beth-nimrah, Succoth, and Zaphon. It included the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon. This was the land east of the Jordan River, up to the edge of the Sea of Chinnereth.
28 This was the land to keep for the family groups of the Gadites. It included the cities and villages.
29 This is what Moses had given to the family groups of the half-tribe of Manasseh. He gave it to half of the family line of Manasseh:
30 The land from Mahanaim through all Bashan — all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan. It included all the towns of Jair that are in Bashan, sixty cities.

31 It included half of Gilead, and Ashtaroth and Edrei, the king's cities of Og in Bashan. All this was for the family groups of the family line of Machir son of Manasseh. It was for half of the family line of Machir.
32 These were the shares Moses had given them on the plains of Moab. He gave them east of the Jordan River, east of Jericho.
33 But Moses gave no land to the tribe of Levi. The LORD, the God of Israel, is their share, just as he had promised them.

title: Joshua 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 These are the shares of land that the Israelites got in Canaan. Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and the family leaders of the tribes of Israel gave them out.
2 They divided the land by lot for the nine and a half tribes. The LORD had told Moses to do it this way.
3 Moses had already given land east of the Jordan to two and a half tribes. But he gave no share of land to the Levites among them.
4 The sons of Joseph became two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim. The Levites got no share of the land. They got only cities to live in, with open fields for their sheep and cattle.
5 So the Israelites did what the LORD had told Moses. And they divided the land.
6 Then the sons of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal. Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, "You know what the LORD said to Moses, the man of God, at Kadesh-barnea about you and me.
7 I was forty years old when Moses, the servant of the LORD, sent me from Kadesh-barnea to look at the land in secret. I brought back to him a true report.
8 My brothers who went with me made the people very afraid. But I stayed loyal to the LORD my God.

9 On that day Moses made a promise to me. He said, 'The land where you walked will be your share. It will belong to you and your children forever. This is because you followed the LORD my God with all your heart.'
10 Now look — the LORD has kept me alive, just as He promised. Forty-five years have passed since He said this to Moses, while Israel walked in the desert. So here I am today, eighty-five years old.
11 I am still as strong today as I was the day Moses sent me out. My strength then is the same now, for war and for daily work.

12 So now, give me this hill land that the LORD promised me on that day. You yourself heard then that the Anakim were there, with great strong cities with walls. Perhaps with the LORD's help, I will push them out, as the LORD has said."
13 Then Joshua blessed Caleb son of Jephunneh. He gave him Hebron as his share of the land.
14 So Hebron belongs to Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite to this day. This is because he followed the LORD, the God of Israel, with all his heart.
15 (Hebron used to be called Kiriath-arba. Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim.) Then the land had peace from war.

title: Joshua 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 The share of land for the tribe of Judah went to the border of Edom. It reached the Wilderness of Zin in the far south.
2 Their southern border started at the south end of the Salt Sea.
3 It went south of the Ascent of Akrabbim and on to Zin. It went south of Kadesh-barnea, past Hezron, up to Addar, and turned to Karka.
4 It went on to Azmon and joined the Brook of Egypt. It ended at the Sea. This was their southern border.
5 The eastern border was the Salt Sea, up to the mouth of the Jordan. The northern border started from the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Jordan.
6 It went up to Beth-hoglah and passed north of Beth-arabah. It went up to the Stone of Bohan, son of Reuben.

7 Then the border went up to Debir from the Valley of Achor. It turned north to Gilgal, which faces the Ascent of Adummim south of the small valley. It went on along the waters of En-shemesh and came out at En-rogel.

8 From there the border went up the Valley of Hinnom. It passed the southern slope of the Jebusites (that is, Jerusalem). Then it went up to the top of the hill west of the Valley of Hinnom, at the north end of the Valley of Rephaim.
9 From the hilltop the border curved to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah. It went on to the cities of Mount Ephron. Then it bent around toward Baalah (that is, Kiriath-jearim).
10 The border curved west from Baalah to Mount Seir. It ran along the northern slope of Mount Jearim (that is, Chesalon). It went down to Beth-shemesh and crossed to Timnah.
11 Then it went to the northern slope of Ekron. It curved toward Shikkeron, passed Mount Baalah, went on to Jabneel, and ended at the Sea.
12 The western border was the coast of the Great Sea. These were the borders around the family groups of Judah.
13 The LORD told Joshua to give Caleb son of Jephunneh a portion among the sons of Judah. He gave him Kiriath-arba, that is, Hebron. (Arba was the father of the Anak family.)
14 Caleb forced out the three sons of Anak from there. They were Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, the children of Anak.
15 From there he marched against the people living in Debir. Debir was known before as Kiriath-sepher.
16 Caleb said, "I will give my daughter Acsah in marriage to the man who attacks and takes Kiriath-sepher."
17 Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb's brother, took the city. So Caleb gave him his daughter Acsah in marriage.
18 One day Acsah came to Othniel. She asked him to ask her father for a field. When she got down from her donkey, Caleb asked her, "What do you want?"
19 She answered, "Give me a blessing. You have given me land in the Negev. Please give me springs of water too." So Caleb gave her both the upper and lower springs.
20 This is the land given to the family groups of the tribe of Judah.
21 These were the cities farthest south in the tribe of Judah, in the Negev, toward the border of Edom:
22 Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur, Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,
23 Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,
24 Ziph, Telem, Bealoth,
25 Hazor-hadattah, Kerioth-hezron (that is, Hazor),
26 Amam, Shema, Moladah,
27 Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, Beth-pelet,
28 Hazar-shual, Beersheba, Biziothiah,
29 Baalah, Iim, Ezem,
30 Eltolad, Chesil, Hormah,
31 Ziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,
32 Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon — twenty-nine cities in all, with their villages.
33 These were in the low hills:
34 Eshtaol, Zorah, Ashnah, Zanoah, En-gannim, Tappuah, Enam,
35 Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, Azekah,
36 Shaaraim, Adithaim, and Gederah (or Gederothaim) — fourteen cities, with their villages.
37 Zenan, Hadashah, Migdal-gad,
38 Dilan, Mizpeh, Joktheel,
39 Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,
40 Cabbon, Lahmas, Chitlish,
41 Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah — sixteen cities, with their villages.
42 Libnah, Ether, Ashan,
43 Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,
44 Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah — nine cities, with their villages.
45 Ekron, with its towns and villages;
46 from Ekron to the sea, all the cities near Ashdod, with their villages;
47 Ashdod, with its towns and villages; Gaza, with its towns and villages, as far as the Brook of Egypt and the coast of the Great Sea.
48 These were in the hill country:
49 Shamir, Jattir, Socoh, Dannah, Kiriath-sannah (that is, Debir),
50 Anab, Eshtemoh, Anim,
51 Goshen, Holon, and Giloh — eleven cities, with their villages.
52 Arab, Dumah, Eshan,
53 Janim, Beth-tappuah, Aphekah,
54 Humtah, Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), and Zior — nine cities, with their villages.
55 Maon, Carmel, Ziph, Juttah,
56 Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,
57 Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah — ten cities, with their villages.
58 Halhul, Beth-zur, Gedor,
59 Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon — six cities, with their villages.
60 Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), and Rabbah — two cities, with their villages.
61 These were in the desert:
62 Beth-arabah, Middin, Secacah, Nibshan, the City of Salt, and En-gedi — six cities, with their villages.
63 But the people of Judah could not force out the Jebusites living in Jerusalem. So the Jebusites still live there among the people of Judah today.

title: Joshua 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 The sons of Joseph received their share of land. It started at the Jordan near Jericho. It reached the waters of Jericho on the east. It went through the desert up to the hills around Bethel.
2 From Bethel (also called Luz), the border went on to Ataroth. There the Archites lived.
3 Then it went down west to the land of the Japhletites. It reached Lower Beth-horon and Gezer. It ended at the sea.
4 So Ephraim and Manasseh, the sons of Joseph, received their land.
5 This was the land for the sons of Ephraim, family by family. Their border started at Ataroth-addar in the east. It reached Upper Beth-horon.
6 Then it went out toward the sea. From Michmethath on the north, it turned to the east. It went toward Taanath-shiloh and passed it to Janoah on the east.
7 From Janoah, the border went down to Ataroth and Naarah. Then it reached Jericho and came out at the Jordan.
8 From Tappuah, the border went west to Kanah Brook. It ended at the sea. This was the land of the tribe of Ephraim, family by family.
9 They also had cities and villages given to them inside the land of Manasseh.
10 But they did not force out the Canaanites in Gezer. So the Canaanites still live among the people of Ephraim even today. But they must work as forced workers.

title: Joshua 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 This was the share of land for the tribe of Manasseh. He was Joseph's oldest son. Machir was the oldest son of Manasseh. He was the father of the Gileadites. He had received Gilead and Bashan because Machir was a strong fighter.

2 This share was also for the rest of Manasseh's family. They were the clans of Abiezer, Helek, Asriel, Shechem, Hepher, and Shemida. These were the other male family of the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph.
3 But Zelophehad had no sons, only daughters. He was the son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh. His daughters were named Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

4 They came to Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and the leaders. They said, "The LORD told Moses to give us land among our brothers." So Joshua gave them land among their father's brothers, as the LORD had said.
5 So Manasseh got ten parts of land. He also got the land of Gilead and Bashan on the other side of the Jordan.
6 The daughters of Manasseh got land among his sons. The land of Gilead went to the rest of Manasseh's sons.
7 The border of Manasseh went from Asher to Michmethath near Shechem. Then it went south to the people who lived in En-tappuah.
8 The area of Tappuah belonged to Manasseh. But Tappuah town itself, on the border, belonged to Ephraim.
9 From there the border went south to Kanah Brook. Some cities of Ephraim were among the cities of Manasseh. But Manasseh's border was on the north side of the brook. It ended at the sea.
10 Ephraim's land was on the south. Manasseh's land was on the north. The sea was its border. It was next to Asher on the north and Issachar on the east.
11 Inside Issachar and Asher, Manasseh was given Beth-shean, Ibleam, Dor (also called Naphath), Endor, Taanach, and Megiddo. Each city had nearby villages.
12 But Manasseh's family could not take over these cities. The Canaanites wanted to stay in this land.
13 When the Israelites grew stronger, they made the Canaanites work as forced workers. But they did not force them out completely.
14 Then the sons of Joseph said to Joshua, "Why have you given us only one share of land? We have many people. The LORD has given us many blessings."
15 Joshua answered, "If you have so many people, the hills of Ephraim are too small. Then go to the forest. Cut down trees and clear an area in the land of the Perizzites and the Rephaim."
16 They replied, "The hills are not enough for us. All the Canaanites in the valley have iron war wagons. They live in Beth-shean and its towns and in the Valley of Jezreel."
17 So Joshua said to the house of Joseph — to Ephraim and Manasseh, "You have many people and great strength. You will not have just one share of land.
18 The hill country will also be yours. It is a forest, but you can clear it. Its far edges will be yours. The Canaanites have iron war wagons and are strong. But you can force them out."

title: Joshua 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then all the people of Israel came together at Shiloh. They set up the Tent of Meeting there. They had taken the land.
2 But seven tribes of Israel had not yet received their share of land.
3 So Joshua said to the Israelites, "How long will you wait to take the land? The LORD, the God of your fathers, has given it to you.
4 Choose three men from each tribe. I will send them out to look at the land and write it down. They will show each tribe's share. Then they will come back to me.
5 They will divide the land into seven parts. Judah will stay in its land in the south. The house of Joseph will stay in its land in the north.
6 When you have written down the seven parts and brought it to me, I will cast lots for you here before the LORD our God.

7 But the Levites have no share among you. Their share is serving the LORD as priests. Gad, Reuben, and half the tribe of Manasseh have already received their share. Moses the servant of the LORD gave it to them east of the Jordan River."
8 The men got up to go out. Joshua told them to write down the land. He said, "Go and look at the land. Write it down. Then come back to me. I will cast lots for you here in Shiloh before the LORD."
9 So the men went out through the land. They wrote down each city in seven parts. Then they came back to Joshua at the camp in Shiloh.
10 Joshua cast lots for them before the LORD at Shiloh. There he gave the land to the Israelites by their groups.
11 The first lot came up for the family groups of the tribe of Benjamin. Their land lay between the tribes of Judah and Joseph.
12 On the north side, the border started at the Jordan. It went up past the north side of Jericho. It went west through the hills. It came out at the desert of Beth-aven.
13 From there the border crossed to the south side of Luz (also called Bethel). It went down to Ataroth-addar on the hill south of Lower Beth-horon.
14 On the west side, the border turned south from the hill facing Beth-horon. It came out at Kiriath-baal (also called Kiriath-jearim), a city of the sons of Judah. This was the west side.
15 On the south side, the border started at the edge of Kiriath-jearim. It went west to the spring at the Waters of Nephtoah.

16 Then it went down to the foot of the hill facing the Valley of Hinnom. This hill is at the north end of the Valley of Rephaim. The border ran down the Valley of Hinnom toward the south side of the Jebusites. It went down to En-rogel.
17 From there it turned north and went on to En-shemesh. It went on to Geliloth, facing the Ascent of Adummim. It went down to the Stone of Bohan son of Reuben.
18 Then it went on to the north side of Beth-arabah and went down into the valley.
19 The border went on to the north side of Beth-hoglah. It came out at the north end of the Salt Sea, at the mouth of the Jordan River. This was the south border.
20 On the east side, the border was the Jordan River. These were the borders around the land of the family groups of the tribe of Benjamin.
21 These were the cities of the family groups of the tribe of Benjamin:
22 Jericho, Beth-hoglah, Emek-keziz, Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, Bethel,
23 Avvim, Parah, Ophrah,
24 Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba — twelve cities, with their villages.
25 Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,
26 Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah,
27 Rekem, Irpeel, Taralah,
28 Zelah, Haeleph, Jebus (also called Jerusalem), Gibeah, and Kiriath-jearim — fourteen cities, with their villages. This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Benjamin.

title: Joshua 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 The second lot came out for the family groups of the tribe of Simeon.
2 Their share of land was inside the land of Judah. It included Beersheba (also called Sheba), Moladah,
3 Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem,
4 Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,
5 Ziklag, Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susah,
6 Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen — thirteen cities, with their villages.
7 Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan — four cities, with their villages,
8 and all the villages around these cities as far as Baalath-beer (Ramah of the Negev). This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Simeon.
9 The share of the Simeonites came from the land of Judah. Judah's share was too big for them. So the Simeonites got their share inside Judah's land.
10 The third lot came up for the family groups of the tribe of Zebulun.
11 The border of their share reached as far as Sarid. It went up west to Maralah, reached Dabbesheth, and met the small river east of Jokneam.
12 From Sarid it turned east along the border of Chisloth-tabor. It went on to Daberath and up to Japhia.
13 From there it crossed east to Gath-hepher and Eth-kazin. It went on to Rimmon and curved toward Neah.
14 Then the border went around the north side of Neah to Hannathon. It ended at the Valley of Iphtah-el.
15 It also included Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem. There were twelve cities, with their villages.
16 This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Zebulun, with these cities and their villages.
17 The fourth lot came out for the family groups of the tribe of Issachar.
18 Their land included Jezreel, Chesulloth, Shunem,
19 Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,
20 Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,
21 Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.
22 The border reached Tabor, Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh. It ended at the Jordan. There were sixteen cities, with their villages.
23 This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Issachar, with these cities and their villages.
24 The fifth lot came out for the family groups of the tribe of Asher.
25 Their land included Helkath, Hali, Beten, Achshaph,
26 Allammelech, Amad, and Mishal. On the west the border touched Carmel and Shihor-libnath.
27 Then it turned east toward Beth-dagon. It touched Zebulun and the Valley of Iphtah-el. It went north to Beth-emek and Neiel, passing Cabul on the left.
28 It went on to Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, and Kanah, as far as Greater Sidon.
29 The border then turned back toward Ramah, as far as the strong city of Tyre. It turned toward Hosah and came out at the Sea near Achzib,
30 Ummah, Aphek, and Rehob. There were twenty-two cities, with their villages.
31 This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Asher, with these cities and their villages.
32 The sixth lot came out for the family groups of the tribe of Naphtali.
33 Their border started at Heleph and the great tree of Zaanannim. It passed Adami-nekeb and Jabneel as far as Lakkum. It ended at the Jordan.
34 Then the border turned west to Aznoth-tabor. From there it ran to Hukkok. It touched Zebulun on the south, Asher on the west, and Judah at the Jordan on the east.
35 The strong cities were Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, Chinnereth,
36 Adamah, Ramah, Hazor,
37 Kedesh, Edrei, En-hazor,
38 Iron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh. There were nineteen cities, with their villages.
39 This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Naphtali, with these cities and their villages.
40 The seventh lot came out for the family groups of the tribe of Dan.
41 The land of their share included Zorah, Eshtaol, Ir-shemesh,
42 Shaalabbin, Aijalon, Ithlah,
43 Elon, Timnah, Ekron,
44 Eltekeh, Gibbethon, Baalath,
45 Jehud, Bene-berak, Gath-rimmon,
46 Me-jarkon, and Rakkon, with the land across from Joppa.

47 Later the Danites lost their land. So they went up and fought against Leshem. They captured it and killed everyone with the sword. They took Leshem, lived there, and called it Dan after their father.
48 This was the share of land for the family groups of the tribe of Dan, with these cities and their villages.
49 When they had finished sharing the land, the Israelites gave Joshua son of Nun a share among them.
50 They did it as the LORD had said. They gave him the city of Timnath-serah in the hills of Ephraim, as he asked. He built up the city and lived in it.

51 These are the shares of land that Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and the family leaders gave to the tribes of Israel by lot. They did it at Shiloh before the LORD, at the door of the Tent of Meeting. So they finished sharing out the land.

title: Joshua 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD spoke to Joshua. He said,
2 "Tell the Israelites to choose the cities of refuge. I told Moses about this before.
3 Anyone who kills another person by accident can run there. These cities will keep him safe from the avenger of blood.
4 A person who runs to one of these cities will stand at the city gate. He will tell his story to the elders. They will bring him into the city. They will give him a place to live.
5 The avenger of blood may chase him. But the elders must not hand the killer over. The man killed his neighbor by accident. He did not hate him before.

6 The killer must stay in that city. He must wait for the people to judge his case. He must also wait until the high priest of that time dies. Then he can go back to his own home, in the town he ran from."

7 So they set apart Kedesh in Galilee, in the hill country of Naphtali. They set apart Shechem in the hill country of Ephraim. They set apart Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the hill country of Judah.

8 East of the Jordan River, near Jericho, they chose three more cities. They chose Bezer on the high desert land, from the tribe of Reuben. They chose Ramoth in Gilead, from the tribe of Gad. They chose Golan in Bashan, from the tribe of Manasseh.

9 These cities were for all the Israelites and the foreigners who lived with them. Anyone who killed a person by accident could run there. He would be safe from the avenger of blood until the people judged his case.

title: Joshua 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 The family leaders of the Levites came to Eleazar the priest. They also came to Joshua son of Nun and the leaders of the other tribes.
2 They met at Shiloh in the land of Canaan. They said, "The LORD told Moses to give us cities to live in. He also said to give us open fields for our farm animals."
3 So the Israelites obeyed the LORD. They gave the Levites cities and open fields from their own share of the land.
4 The first lot came out for the Kohathite clans. The Levites from Aaron's family received thirteen cities. These came from the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.
5 The rest of Kohath's family received ten cities. These came from the tribes of Ephraim, Dan, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
6 Gershon's family received thirteen cities. These came from the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and the half-tribe of Manasseh in Bashan.
7 Merari's family received twelve cities. These came from the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
8 So the Israelites gave the Levites these cities and open fields. They did just as the LORD had told Moses.
9 From the tribes of Judah and Simeon, they chose these cities by name.
10 They gave them to Aaron's family from the Kohathite clans of the Levites. The first lot fell to them.
11 They gave them Kiriath-arba (also called Hebron), with its open fields. It is in the hill country of Judah. (Arba was the father of Anak.)
12 But they had already given the fields and villages around the city to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
13 So they gave Aaron's family these cities with their open fields: Hebron (a city of refuge for the killer), Libnah,
14 Jattir, Eshtemoa,
15 Holon, Debir,
16 Ain, Juttah, and Beth-shemesh. That was nine cities from these two tribes, with their open fields.
17 From the tribe of Benjamin they gave them Gibeon, Geba,
18 Anathoth, and Almon. That was four cities, with their open fields.
19 In all, the priests from Aaron's family received thirteen cities with their open fields.
20 The rest of the Kohathite clans of the Levites received these cities:
21 From the tribe of Ephraim they were given Shechem in the hill country of Ephraim. Shechem was a city of refuge for the killer. They also received Gezer,
22 Kibzaim, and Beth-horon. That was four cities, with their open fields.
23 From the tribe of Dan they were given Elteke, Gibbethon,
24 Aijalon, and Gath-rimmon. That was four cities, with their open fields.
25 From the half-tribe of Manasseh they were given Taanach and Gath-rimmon. That was two cities, with their open fields.
26 In all, the rest of the Kohathite clans received ten cities, with their open fields.

27 The Gershonite clans of the Levites were given these cities: From the half-tribe of Manasseh, they received Golan in Bashan (a city of refuge for the killer) and Beeshterah. That was two cities, with their open fields.
28 From the tribe of Issachar they were given Kishion, Daberath,
29 Jarmuth, and En-gannim. That was four cities, with their open fields.
30 From the tribe of Asher they were given Mishal, Abdon,
31 Helkath, and Rehob. That was four cities, with their open fields.
32 From the tribe of Naphtali they were given Kedesh in Galilee (a city of refuge for the killer), Hammoth-dor, and Kartan. That was three cities, with their open fields.
33 In all, the Gershonite clans received thirteen cities, with their open fields.
34 The Merarite clan (the rest of the Levites) were given these cities:
35 From the tribe of Zebulun they were given Jokneam, Kartah, Dimnah, and Nahalal. That was four cities, with their open fields.
36 From the tribe of Reuben they were given Bezer, Jahaz,
37 Kedemoth, and Mephaath. That was four cities, with their open fields.
38 From the tribe of Gad they were given Ramoth in Gilead (a city of refuge for the killer), Mahanaim,
39 Heshbon, and Jazer. That was four cities in all, with their open fields.
40 In all, the Merarite clans (the rest of the Levites) received twelve cities.
41 So the Levites received forty-eight cities in all, with their open fields. These were inside the land of the Israelites.
42 Each of these cities had its own open fields around it. This was true for all the cities.
43 So the LORD gave Israel all the land. He had promised it to their fathers long ago. The people took the land and lived in it.
44 The LORD gave them peace from all sides. He had promised this to their fathers. No enemy could stand against them. The LORD gave all their enemies into their hand.
45 Not one of the LORD's good promises to Israel failed. Every promise came true.

title: Joshua 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then Joshua called together the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
2 He said to them, "You have done all that Moses the servant of the LORD told you. You have obeyed my words in all I commanded.
3 All this time, you have not left your brothers. You have kept the duty the LORD your God gave you.
4 Now the LORD your God has given your brothers peace, as He promised. So you may go back to your homes. Moses gave you land across the Jordan.

5 But take great care to obey the command and the law. Moses the servant of the LORD gave them to you. Love the LORD your God. Walk in all His ways. Keep His commands. Hold on to Him. Serve Him with all your heart and all your soul."
6 So Joshua blessed them and sent them away. And they went to their homes.
7 Moses had given land in Bashan to half the tribe of Manasseh. Joshua gave land west of the Jordan to the other half, with their brothers. When Joshua sent them home, he blessed them.
8 He said, "Go home with your great wealth. Take your large herds of animals. Take silver, gold, bronze, iron, and many clothes. Share with your brothers the things you took from your enemies."

9 So the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh left the other Israelites at Shiloh in Canaan. They went back to their own land of Gilead. They had received this land by the command of the LORD through Moses.
10 They came to Geliloth near the Jordan, in the land of Canaan. There the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh built a very big altar by the Jordan.

11 Then the other Israelites heard this news. People said, "Look! The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built an altar. It stands at the border of Canaan, at Geliloth near the Jordan, on the Israelite side."
12 When they heard this, all the people of Israel met at Shiloh. They got ready to go to war against them.
13 The Israelites sent Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest to the land of Gilead. He went to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
14 With him they sent ten chiefs. Each chief was a family leader from one tribe of Israel. Each was the head of a family among the clans.
15 They came to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead. They said to them,

16 "All the people of the LORD ask this: 'Why have you been unfaithful to the God of Israel today? You have turned away from the LORD. You have built your own altar. You have turned against the LORD this day.
17 Was not the sin at Peor enough for us? We are still not pure from it today. That sin brought a sickness from God on His people.
18 And now, will you turn away from the LORD? If you turn against the LORD today, He will be angry with all Israel tomorrow.

19 Maybe your land is not pure for worship. Then cross over to the land of the LORD. The tent of the LORD stands there. Take some land among us. But do not turn against the LORD or against us. Do not build your own altar instead of the altar of the LORD our God.
20 Remember Achan son of Zerah. He was unfaithful about the things God said to destroy. So God's strong anger came on all Israel. Achan was not the only one who died for his sin!'"
21 Then the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh answered the leaders of the clans of Israel.

22 They said, "The LORD, the Mighty One, is God! The LORD, the Mighty One, is God! He knows the truth, and let Israel also know. If we turned against the LORD or were unfaithful, do not save us today.
23 Did we build an altar to turn from Him? Did we build it for burnt offerings or grain offerings? Did we build it for peace offerings? Then let the LORD Himself judge us.
24 No, we did this for one reason. We feared that one day your children might say to ours, 'What do you have to do with the LORD, the God of Israel?
25 The LORD has put the Jordan as a border between us and you Reubenites and Gadites. You have no part in the LORD!' So your children might stop our children from fearing the LORD.
26 That is why we said, 'Let us build an altar for ourselves. But it is not for burnt offerings or sacrifices.

27 Let it be proof between us and you, and for all our children to come. Then we will worship the LORD before Him with our burnt offerings, sacrifices, and peace offerings. And in the future your children cannot say to ours, You have no part in the LORD!'

28 We also said, 'If they ever say this to us or to our children, we will answer them. We will say: Look at the copy of the altar of the LORD. Our fathers made it. It is not for burnt offerings or sacrifices. It is proof between us and you.'
