29 We would never turn against the LORD. We would never turn from Him today by building another altar. Such an altar would be for burnt offerings, grain offerings, or sacrifices. The true altar of the LORD our God stands before His tent."

30 Phinehas the priest and the chiefs of the people heard these words. They were the heads of Israel's clans. The descendants of Reuben, Gad, and Manasseh had spoken well. So the leaders were satisfied.
31 Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest said to them, "Today we know that the LORD is among us. You have not been unfaithful to Him. So you have saved the Israelites from the LORD's anger."
32 Then Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest and the leaders went back. They returned to the Israelites in the land of Canaan. They brought back the report about the Reubenites and Gadites in Gilead.
33 The Israelites were happy with the report. They blessed God. They did not talk about going to war anymore. They did not plan to destroy the land where the Reubenites and Gadites lived.
34 So the Reubenites and Gadites named the altar "Witness." They said, "It is proof between us that the LORD is God."

title: Joshua 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 A long time passed. The LORD had given Israel peace from all their enemies. Joshua was now very old.
2 He called together all Israel — the elders, leaders, judges, and officers. He said, "I am very old now.
3 You have seen everything that the LORD your God did to all these nations for you. The LORD your God fought for you.

4 Look, I have given your tribes the land of these nations that are left. I have also given you the land of the nations I already defeated. This is from the Jordan in the east to the Great Sea in the west.
5 The LORD your God will push them out of your way. He will send them away from you. Then you will take their land, just as the LORD your God promised you.
6 So be very strong. Keep and obey everything written in the Book of the Law of Moses. Do not turn from it, left or right.
7 Do not mix with these nations that are still among you. Do not pray to their gods. Do not make promises by their names. Do not serve them or bow down to them.
8 Instead, hold on to the LORD your God, as you have done until today.
9 The LORD has driven out big and strong nations before you. To this day, no one can stand against you.
10 One of you can chase away a thousand enemies. This is because the LORD your God fights for you, just as He promised.
11 So be very careful. Love the LORD your God.
12 Do not turn away and join with the rest of these nations among you. Do not marry their people or mix with them.

13 If you do, know this for sure: the LORD your God will not drive these nations out for you anymore. Instead, they will be a trap for you. They will be a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes. You will die from this good land that the LORD your God has given you.
14 Now look, today I am going to die, like all people. You know in your heart and soul that every good promise of the LORD your God has come true. Every promise was kept. Not one promise failed.
15 Every good thing the LORD your God promised has come true. In the same way, the LORD will bring trouble on you that He warned about. He will destroy you from this good land He has given you.

16 This will happen if you break the covenant of the LORD your God. He commanded you to keep it. If you go and serve other gods and bow down to them, the LORD will be very angry with you. Then you will quickly die from this good land He has given you."

title: Joshua 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joshua
paragraphs: 1 Then Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel at Shechem. He called the elders, leaders, judges, and officers of Israel. They stood before God.

2 Joshua said to all the people, "This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'Long ago your fathers lived across the Euphrates River. This included Terah, the father of Abraham and Nahor. They worshiped other gods.
3 But I took your father Abraham from across the Euphrates. I led him through all the land of Canaan. I gave him many children. I gave him Isaac.
4 To Isaac I gave Jacob and Esau. I gave Esau Mount Seir to own. But Jacob and his sons went down to Egypt.
5 Then I sent Moses and Aaron. I brought trouble on the Egyptians by what I did there. After that, I brought you out.
6 I brought your fathers out of Egypt. You came to the Red Sea. The Egyptians chased them with chariots and horsemen to the Red Sea.

7 So your fathers cried out to the LORD. He put darkness between you and the Egyptians. He brought the sea over them and covered them. Your own eyes saw what I did to the Egyptians. Then you lived in the wilderness for a long time.
8 Later, I brought you to the land of the Amorites across the Jordan. They fought against you. But I gave them to you, and you took their land. I destroyed them before you.
9 Then Balak son of Zippor, the king of Moab, came to fight against Israel. He sent for Balaam son of Beor to curse you.
10 But I would not listen to Balaam. So he blessed you again and again. I saved you from his hand.

11 After this, you crossed the Jordan and came to Jericho. The people of Jericho fought against you. So did the Amorites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hittites, Girgashites, Hivites, and Jebusites. I gave them all to you.
12 I sent the hornet ahead of you. It pushed out the two Amorite kings before you. You did not win by your own sword or bow.
13 So I gave you a land you did not work for. I gave you cities you did not build. Now you live in them. You eat from vineyards and olive trees you did not plant.'
14 Now then, fear the LORD and serve Him with honest and true hearts. Throw away the gods your fathers served across the Euphrates and in Egypt. Serve the LORD.

15 But if you do not want to serve the LORD, then **Choose this day whom you will serve**. Will you serve the gods your fathers served across the Euphrates? Or will you serve the gods of the Amorites in whose land you live? **But as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD!**"
16 The people replied, "We will never leave the LORD to serve other gods!

17 The LORD our God brought us and our fathers out of the land of Egypt. He brought us out of the land of slavery. He did these great signs before our eyes. He kept us safe on all our journey, among all the nations we passed through.
18 The LORD pushed out before us all the nations, including the Amorites who lived in the land. We too will serve the LORD, because He is our God!"
19 But Joshua said to the people, "You are not able to serve the LORD. He is a holy God. He is a jealous God. He will not forgive your rebellion or your sins.
20 If you leave the LORD and serve foreign gods, He will turn and bring trouble on you. He will destroy you, even after He has been good to you."
21 "No!" replied the people. "We will serve the LORD!"
22 Then Joshua told them, "You are witnesses against yourselves. You have chosen to serve the LORD." "We are witnesses!" they said.
23 "Now then," he said, "throw away the foreign gods among you. Turn your hearts to the LORD, the God of Israel."
24 So the people said to Joshua, "We will serve the LORD our God and obey His voice."
25 On that day Joshua made a covenant for the people. There at Shechem he gave them rules and laws.
26 Joshua wrote these things in the Book of the Law of God. Then he took a large stone and set it up there. He placed it under the oak near the holy place of the LORD.

27 Joshua said to all the people, "Look at this stone. It will be a witness against us. It has heard all the words the LORD has spoken to us. It will be a witness against you if you ever deny your God."
28 Then Joshua sent the people home, each to his own inheritance.
29 Some time later, Joshua son of Nun, the servant of the LORD, died at the age of 110.
30 They buried him in the land of his inheritance. The place was Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
31 Israel served the LORD all the days of Joshua. They also served Him all the days of the elders who lived longer than Joshua. These elders had seen all the works the LORD had done for Israel.

32 The bones of Joseph were buried at Shechem. The Israelites had brought them up out of Egypt. The place was the plot of land that Jacob had bought from the sons of Hamor, the father of Shechem, for a hundred pieces of silver. So it became an inheritance for Joseph's descendants.
33 Eleazar son of Aaron also died. They buried him at Gibeah, which had been given to his son Phinehas in the hill country of Ephraim.
